text.title: Automated Reporting and Delivery
text.summary: Scheduled report configurations, Slack Block Kit delivery, report templates, and BI integration patterns.

rule.statement: Run three report cadences: daily standup digest (09:00 weekdays, Slack), weekly status report (Friday 16:00, email to stakeholders), sprint report (on sprint-end event, PDF).
rule.rationale: Each cadence serves a distinct audience: daily digest for the team, weekly status for stakeholders, sprint report for retrospectives. Mixing cadences produces reports nobody reads.

rule.statement: Add a 1-hour buffer when querying at sprint boundaries — query the previous closed sprint explicitly rather than relying on "current sprint" logic immediately after sprint close.
rule.rationale: Sprint transition state is inconsistent for up to 1 hour after close; metrics computed at the boundary may mix old and new sprint data.

rule.statement: Validate every metric query before rendering: if a required metric returns null or unexpectedly zero results, emit a data quality alert rather than a report showing zeros.
rule.rationale: A sprint with 0 items in progress is almost always a data quality problem, not reality; misleading zeros cause stakeholders to act on false signals.

antipattern.description: Silently showing 0% completion when JQL returns no results.
antipattern.reason: Downstream readers assume 0 means "nothing done" rather than "query returned nothing"; add explicit null checks before rendering.

rule.statement: Limit Slack Block Kit text blocks to under 3000 characters per block; chunk large tables into multiple messages or link to a full report rather than inlining all rows.
rule.rationale: Slack enforces a 3001-character limit per text block; exceeding it silently truncates the message without an error to the sender.

section.title: BI Integration

rule.statement: Use idempotent upserts when writing to a metrics database; archive every generated report to a persistent store (Notion page, S3, GitHub issue) — Slack channel history disappears.
rule.rationale: Cron overlap can trigger duplicate runs; idempotent upserts prevent duplicate rows. Slack messages are unreliable as report archives; persistent stores allow audit and replay.

rule.statement: BI connections: PM tool APIs use tool-specific query syntax — Jira uses status = Done, Linear uses completedAt != null, ClickUp uses status = complete. Never use a generic abstraction layer that hides this.
rule.rationale: Generic PM wrappers drift out of sync with tool API changes; tool-specific queries remain correct and debuggable.

section.reference: JQL query collection for common dashboard metrics
